content.thinking:
Let me inspect the current CockroachDB cluster configuration to understand what non-default settings are in place, then write a rollback script and save it as a ConfigMap.

I'll run the three inspection commands in parallel.

content.text: I'll inspect the current cluster configuration across all three dimensions in parallel.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT variable, value FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE value != default_value;" 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT target, raw_config_sql FROM crdb_internal.zones;" 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}' 2>&1

content.thinking: Good. There are TLS secrets. I need to exec into the pod and use the certificates. Let me look at the pod to see how it's configured and where the certs are.

input.description: List cert files in the pod

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT variable, value FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE value != default_value;" 2>&1
input.description: Query non-default cluster settings with TLS certs

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT target, raw_config_sql FROM crdb_internal.zones;" 2>&1
input.description: Query zone configurations with TLS certs
